note: Jumper et al., 'Highly accurate protein structure prediction with AlphaFold', Nature 596, 583-589 (2021), DOI 10.1038/s41586-021-03819-2. Read fully and BLIND before seeing any manuscript claim. AlphaFold is a SPECIALIZED SINGLE-TASK PREDICTOR: it maps one input (amino-acid sequence + MSA of homologues + optional templates) to one output (3D atomic coordinates). Architecture: genetic/structure DB search -> MSA+pair representations -> Evoformer trunk (48 blocks) -> structure module (8 shared-weight blocks) -> 3D structure, then Amber relaxation (cleanup only, does not improve accuracy metrics). It has NO hypothesis-experiment-validation cycle, NO external tool/oracle querying at inference, NO lab automation, NO autonomous generate-evaluate-select discovery loop. The only iteration is 'recycling' (feed whole-network output back into the same modules 3 times / 4 passes) -- INTERNAL refinement of one structural hypothesis inside the forward pass, explicitly analogized to iterative refinement in computer vision; and per-layer 'search/rearrange' language which is metaphorical representation refinement across network depth. Self-distillation is a TRAINING-TIME generate-then-filter procedure, not an inference discovery loop. The manuscript (C033) uses jumper2021alphafold as an EXCLUSION example ('specialized predictors without a generation-and-search loop, e.g. AlphaFold'). The digest CONFIRMS this characterization exactly. CASP14 headline for reference: median backbone 0.96 A r.m.s.d.95 (95% CI 0.85-1.16) vs next-best 2.8 A; all-atom 1.5 A vs 3.5 A.

C033.evidence: Manuscript L73 (CHAR, scope-definition): 'We include systems whose primary goal is automated scientific \emph{discovery} through hypothesis-experiment-validation cycles, and exclude specialized predictors without a generation-and-search loop (e.g., AlphaFold~\citep{jumper2021alphafold}).' AlphaFold is cited as the canonical EXCLUSION -- a specialized predictor lacking a generation-and-search discovery loop -- and the paper substantiates every element of that descriptor. (1) SPECIALIZED PREDICTOR: 'The AlphaFold network directly predicts the 3D coordinates of all heavy atoms for a given protein using the primary amino acid sequence and aligned sequences of homologues as inputs' (p.584-585). It performs ONE prediction task (sequence -> structure); Reporting Summary confirms 'no experimental work is described in this study; the results are the output of a computational method' (p. Reporting Summary). (2) NO HYPOTHESIS-EXPERIMENT-VALIDATION CYCLE / NO GENERATION-AND-SEARCH LOOP: the pipeline is a single feedforward map -- genetic/structure DB search -> Evoformer (48 blocks) -> structure module (8 blocks) -> 3D structure (Fig. 1e). There is no proposal of candidate hypotheses evaluated against an external oracle and iterated. The only iteration is internal 'recycling': 'we reinforce the notion of iterative refinement by repeatedly applying the final loss to outputs and then feeding the outputs recursively into the same modules. The iterative refinement using the whole network (which we term recycling and is related to approaches in computer vision)' (p.585) -- i.e. refinement of one structural hypothesis inside the forward pass, three times (Fig. 4b), NOT a generate-evaluate-select discovery cycle. Even the paper's 'the network searches and rearranges secondary structure elements for many layers' (p.587) is metaphorical internal representation refinement across depth, not an external search over hypotheses/experiments. Self-distillation ('use a trained network to predict the structure of around 350,000 diverse sequences from Uniclust30 ... train the same architecture again from scratch', p.587) is a TRAINING-TIME procedure, not an inference-time discovery loop. Therefore excluding AlphaFold from a corpus of automated-discovery (hypothesis-experiment-validation) systems, on the stated ground that it is a specialized predictor without a generation-and-search loop, is fully faithful to the paper. jumper2021alphafold is exactly the right, uncontroversial exemplar for this exclusion.

reverse_findings.fact: AlphaFold's only iterative mechanism is 'recycling': the whole-network output is fed recursively back into the same modules three times (4 passes total), described as 'iterative refinement ... related to approaches in computer vision' (p.585; trajectory of 192 = 4x48 intermediate structures, Fig. 4b, p.587). The paper also uses search-like language for internal dynamics ('the network searches and rearranges secondary structure elements for many layers', p.587) and a TRAINING-TIME self-distillation step (predict ~350,000 Uniclust30 structures, filter to high-confidence, retrain from scratch, p.587).
reverse_findings.loc: paper p.585 (recycling), p.587 (per-layer search language; self-distillation); manuscript L73 (C033)
reverse_findings.mismatch: None -- CONFIRMS the exclusion. A careless reader might mistake recycling / per-layer 'search' / self-distillation for a 'generation-and-search loop', but all three are internal-refinement or training-time constructs, NOT an inference-time hypothesis-experiment-validation discovery cycle with external evaluation and candidate iteration. The manuscript's characterization ('specialized predictor without a generation-and-search loop') is correct.

reverse_findings.fact: AlphaFold's CASP14 headline accuracy is a median backbone 0.96 A r.m.s.d.95 (Ca r.m.s.d. at 95% residue coverage; 95% CI 0.85-1.16 A) vs the next-best method's 2.8 A (95% CI 2.7-4.0 A); all-atom 1.5 A vs 3.5 A best alternative; n=87 CASP14 domains, shown vs top-15 of 146 entrants (Abstract; p.584; Fig. 1a).
reverse_findings.loc: paper Abstract, p.584, Fig. 1a; manuscript L73 (C033)
reverse_findings.mismatch: None. The manuscript cites AlphaFold only as a categorical EXCLUSION example and attributes NO numeric or accuracy claim to it, so there is no quantitative statement to mis-state. Recorded purely as a cross-reference anchor for the paper's identity.

reverse_findings.fact: The paper explicitly scopes itself to the structure-PREDICTION component of the protein folding problem and does not claim autonomous discovery, experiment design, or closed-loop iteration; it also does not explicitly model ligands/ions (e.g. correctly places side chains at a zinc site 'even though it does not explicitly predict the zinc ion', Fig. 1c) and its post-prediction Amber relaxation does not improve GDT/lDDT-Ca accuracy.
reverse_findings.loc: paper Abstract, Fig. 1c, End-to-end structure prediction section (p.586-587)
reverse_findings.mismatch: None. Reinforces that AlphaFold is a bounded specialized predictor, consistent with the manuscript's exclusion rationale.
